Front: Longest Repeating Character Replacement: what makes a window valid, and why can max_freq stay 'stale'?
Back: Valid while (window_length - max_freq_in_window) <= k, since window_length - max_freq is how many chars you'd have to replace. Grow right; shrink left when it exceeds k; track max window size. max_freq is never decremented on shrink, and that is fine: res only grows when a genuinely higher max_freq is found, so a stale (too-high) max_freq never produces a wrong (larger) answer. O(n).

Front: MLflow vocabulary: Experiment, Run, Param, Metric, Artifact.
Back: Experiment = a named group of runs. Run = one training execution. Param = an input you chose (a hyperparameter). Metric = an output you measured (accuracy). Artifact = a file produced (the model, a plot). The logged model bundle (MLmodel metadata + model.pkl + env files) is what makes it re-loadable and underpins the Model Registry.

Front: MLOps lesson from comparing hyperparameter variants on iris.
Back: Accuracy plateaued - a small model (depth 3, 50 trees) tied the biggest (depth 8, 200 trees). Capacity has diminishing returns; ship the simplest sufficient model (faster/cheaper to serve). Caveat: a single train/test split on a tiny dataset is noisy - trust differences only with cross-validation and multiple seeds.

Front: Why did MLflow fail to start on Python 3.14, and what's the takeaway?
Back: Python 3.14 removed importlib.abc.Traversable (moved to importlib.resources.abc), and MLflow still imported it the old way -> ImportError. The ML ecosystem lags the newest Python by months; use 3.11/3.12 for ML work and pin the version (match your container's python:3.12).

Front: Kubernetes: what does CreateContainerConfigError usually mean?
Back: A referenced ConfigMap or Secret (or a key inside one) is missing or misnamed, so the container's config can't be assembled - the pod itself is fine, its config wiring points at something absent. Common cause: the manifest wasn't applied (e.g. the file lived outside the folder you ran kubectl apply -f on). The apply output is the receipt - check that every object was created.
